channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [Hacker News] Thomann takes legal action against Fender
item.description: Thomann takes legal action against Fender

item.title: [Latent Space] Why the Frontier Ecosystem must be Open — Matei Zaharia and Reynold Xin, Databricks
item.description: Why the Frontier Ecosystem must be Open — Matei Zaharia and Reynold Xin, Databricks

item.title: [Hacker News] OpenAI unveils its first custom chip, built by Broadcom
item.description: OpenAI unveils its first custom chip, built by Broadcom

item.title: [Hacker News] Computer use in Gemini 3.5 Flash
item.description: Computer use in Gemini 3.5 Flash

item.title: [Google DeepMind] Introducing computer use in Gemini 3.5 Flash
item.description: Introducing computer use in Gemini 3.5 Flash

item.title: [Hacker News] Boffin claims Microsoft's "quantum leap" is invalid due to "basic Python errors"
item.description: Boffin claims Microsoft's "quantum leap" is invalid due to "basic Python errors"

item.title: [Hacker News] For Most of the World, Open-Source AI Is the Only Way Forward
item.description: For Most of the World, Open-Source AI Is the Only Way Forward

item.title: [Hacker News] RubyLLM: A single, beautiful Ruby framework for all major AI providers
item.description: RubyLLM: A single, beautiful Ruby framework for all major AI providers

item.title: [Hacker News] PR spam today looks like email spam in the early 2000s
item.description: PR spam today looks like email spam in the early 2000s

item.title: [Hacker News] OpenAI and Broadcom unveil LLM-optimized inference chip
item.description: OpenAI and Broadcom unveil LLM-optimized inference chip

item.title: [Hacker News] Reid Hoffman says SpaceX 'not an AI company', xAI 'complete train wreck'
item.description: Reid Hoffman says SpaceX 'not an AI company', xAI 'complete train wreck'

item.title: [Anthropic] What 81,000 people told us about the economics of AI
item.description: What 81,000 people told us about the economics of AI

item.title: [Hacker News] Minimus container images are now free
item.description: Minimus container images are now free

item.title: [Hacker News] NSA lost access to Mythos amid Anthropic dispute
item.description: NSA lost access to Mythos amid Anthropic dispute

item.title: [GitHub Trending] apple/container — A tool for creating and running Linux containers using lightweight virtual machines on a Mac. It is written in Swift, and optimized for Apple silicon.
item.description: Apple releases container, a Swift-based tool for running Linux containers via lightweight virtual machines on Apple silicon Macs.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] interviewstreet/hiring-agent — AI agent to evaluate and score resumes.
item.description: Interviewstreet's hiring-agent is an AI agent designed to evaluate and score resumes.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] stablyai/orca — Orca is the ADE for working with a fleet of parallel agents. Run any coding agent with your own subscription. Available on desktop and mobile.
item.description: Orca is an ADE for managing parallel coding agents, available on desktop and mobile with user subscriptions.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] google-labs-code/design.md — A format specification for describing a visual identity to coding agents. DESIGN.md gives agents a persistent, structured understanding of a design system.
item.description: Google Labs released DESIGN.md, a format specification for describing visual identity systems to coding agents.

item.title: [Latent Space] [AINews] Claude Tag: Multiplayer, Proactive, Persistent Agents in Slack
item.description: [AINews] Claude Tag: Multiplayer, Proactive, Persistent Agents in Slack

item.title: [OpenAI] OpenAI and Broadcom unveil LLM-optimized inference chip
item.description: OpenAI and Broadcom unveil LLM-optimized inference chip

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] EXPO-SQL: Execution-based Clause-level Policy Optimization for Text-to-SQL
item.description: EXPO-SQL proposes clause-level rewards for Text-to-SQL reinforcement learning, improving performance over query-level reward approaches.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] ModTGCN: Modularity-aware Graph Neural Networks for Text Classification
item.description: ModTGCN improves text classification by incorporating document graph modularity into graph neural networks, achieving faster training and better performance on complex datasets.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Quantifying Prior Dominance in RAG Systems
item.description: Study finds small language models outperform large ones in RAG factual extraction; large models suffer from prior dominance, overrelying on parametric knowledge.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Self-Recognition Finetuning can Prevent and Reverse Emergent Misalignment
item.description: Self-generated text recognition finetuning effectively prevents and reverses emergent misalignment across multiple language models via character restoration.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Evaluating LLM Usage for Efficient and Explainable Numerical and Classified Implicit Sentiment Analysis of Product Desirability
item.description: LLMs effectively quantify implicit sentiment in product feedback, achieving 94% classification accuracy and 0.97 correlation with expert labels at lower cost than larger models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Ground Then Rank: Revisiting Knowledge-Based VQA with Training-Free Entity Identification
item.description: Training-free identify-before-answer framework improves KB-VQA by decoupling entity identification from evidence ranking, outperforming fine-tuned multi-modal baselines.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] One Year Later...The Harms Persist, But So Do We!
item.description: LLM safeguards fail for most mental health conditions, effectively protecting only against suicide and self-harm risks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Do LLM Attribution Metrics Transfer? Auditing Retrieval-Augmented Generation Evaluation Across Datasets and Constructs
item.description: Study finds automatic attribution metrics for retrieval-augmented generation don't generalize across datasets; performance rankings reverse, requiring per-dataset validation rather than universal selection.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] When Retrieval Metrics Mislead: Measuring Policy Signal in Long-Horizon Tool-Use Agents
item.description: Exact-match retrieval recall is unreliable for measuring policy signal in tool-use agents; downstream performance differs minimally from gold policies.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] QuechuaTok: Morphological Boundary Accuracy as a Necessary Metric for Tokenizer Evaluation in Agglutinative Low-Resource Languages
item.description: QuechuaTok benchmark shows morphological boundary accuracy is essential for tokenizer evaluation in agglutinative languages, with PRPE achieving 83% versus BPE's 6.67%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Layer-wise Probing of wav2vec 2.0 and Whisper for Consonant Cluster Reduction in African American English
item.description: Wav2vec 2.0 and Whisper encode African American English consonant cluster reduction as structured phonological variation rather than simple deletion.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Does My Embedding Reflect That $A = B$? Evaluating Mathematical Equivalence in Embedding Models
item.description: Current embedding models group mathematical statements by terminology rather than underlying meaning; contrastive learning on equivalent statement pairs improves recognition.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Faithful by Construction: Claim-Anchored Attribution for Multi-Document Summarization
item.description: CAMS extracts atomic claims from multiple documents with token-level attribution, producing summaries where every sentence is anchored to verified source spans.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] RASC+: Retrieval-Constrained LLM Adjudication for Clinical Value Set Authoring
item.description: Retrieval-constrained LLM adjudication substantially improves clinical value set completion by combining high-recall candidate pools with auditable code selection.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Towards Spec Learning: Inference-Time Alignment from Preference Pairs
item.description: Spec learning framework uses preference judgments to generate interpretable natural-language prompts for LLM steering without requiring model retraining.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Towards Version-aware Operations and Transaction Memories for Multi-layer MeMo
item.description: MeMo proposes version-aware operations and transaction memories enabling language model knowledge updates without full retraining through explicit memory editing.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Best Preprocessing Techniques for Sentiment Analysis
item.description: Study finds optimal order for text preprocessing techniques in sentiment analysis: tokenization, text cleaning, stemming, and stopword removal.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Selective Capability Unlearning in End-to-End Spoken Language Understanding
item.description: Binding Subspace, a representation-level method, selectively removes specific intents from spoken language understanding models while preserving other learned behaviors.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Sentence-Level Contextual Entrainment in Large Language Models
item.description: Study shows LLMs assign higher probabilities to sentences appearing in prompts; effect controlled by 2-4% of attention heads and can be mitigated without performance loss.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] CAVEWOMAN: How Large Language Models Behave Under Linguistic Input and Output Compression
item.description: Input compression increases net LLM costs and reduces accuracy; output compression cuts costs 1.4-2.4x across API and open-weight models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Predicting Poets' Origins from Verse: A Computational Analysis of Regional Linguistic Fingerprints in the Complete Tang Poems
item.description: Study predicts Tang poets' geographic origins from verse using character n-grams and neural models, revealing linguistic patterns correlating with geographic distance and historical periods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] PORTER: Language-Grounded Event Representations for Portable Structured EHR Foundation Models
item.description: PORTER, a language-grounded EHR model, transfers across institutions without retraining by representing clinical events through natural language descriptions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Metis: Bridging Text and Code Memory for Self-Evolving Agents
item.description: Metis system combines text and code memory with complementary trade-offs in self-evolving agents, improving task accuracy by up to 20.6%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] MedBench v5: A Dynamic, Process-Oriented, and Hallucination-Aware Benchmark for Clinical Multimodal Models
item.description: MedBench v5 introduces a dynamic, process-oriented benchmark for clinical AI models with hallucination detection and information-flow stress testing.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] BehaviorBench: Benchmarking Foundation Models for Behavioral Science Tasks
item.description: BehaviorBench benchmark assesses foundation models on behavioral science tasks, finding specialized behavioral models achieve better population-level alignment than general models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] A P\={a}ninian Foundation for Indic Language Processing
item.description: Study proposes using Pānini's Sanskrit grammar framework to unify NLP tools for Indic languages and improve cross-language transfer.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] A Synthetic Reliability-Aware PINN Benchmark for Offshore Wind Turbine Support-Structure Monitoring with Bayesian Inverse Identification
item.description: Physics-Informed Neural Network benchmark enables efficient structural health monitoring of offshore wind turbines using sparse measurements and Bayesian methods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Aspect-Based Sentiment Evolution and its Correlation with Review Rounds in Multi-Round Peer Reviews: A Deep Learning Approach
item.description: Study analyzes aspect-level sentiments in multi-round peer reviews using deep learning, finding positive sentiment increases with more review rounds.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] MMed-Bench-IR: A Heterogeneous Benchmark for Multilingual Medical Information Retrieval
item.description: MMed-Bench-IR benchmark evaluates multilingual medical information retrieval across six languages, revealing severe performance degradation in non-English retrieval.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Decoherence as Defence and the Magnitude of Noise Regularisation: A Rigorous N -Qubit Theory of Stochastic Quantum Neural Networks for Adversarially Robust Network Intrusion Detection
item.description: Quantum neural networks with depolarising noise achieve adversarial robustness in intrusion detection with provable robustness bounds.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Systematic Exploration of 4-Expert Heterogeneous Mixture-of-Experts via Automated Pipeline Search
item.description: Automated pipeline search discovers ShuffleNet-MobileNetV3 4-Expert MoE ensembles as top performers and identifies bias in search methodology.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Weight-Space Geometry of Offline Reasoning Training
item.description: Six offline reasoning training methods produce similar weight updates except DPO, which achieves highest accuracy despite orthogonal direction.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Survey on Federated Causal Discovery and Inference
item.description: Survey provides systematic taxonomies for federated causal discovery and inference, organizing methods across federation topology, structure learning, and data heterogeneity.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Low-power analogue neural networks with trainable nonlinear connections for continuous control
item.description: Analogue neural networks with trainable nonlinear connections efficiently solve continuous control tasks using fewer nodes, with projected 30-microwatt power consumption.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Synergizing Physically Constrained MCMC and Chemical-Informed Gaussian Processes for Reaction Network Discovery
item.description: Physically-constrained workflow combining MCMC and chemical-informed Gaussian processes enables reaction network discovery from sparse, noisy experimental data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Exploring Dualistic Meta-Learning to Enhance Domain Generalization in Open Set Scenarios
item.description: MEDIC applies dualistic meta-learning to improve open set domain generalization by balancing boundaries across domains and classes simultaneously.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] One Ruler: A Same-Hands Re-Evaluation of Bivariate Causal Direction on Tuebingen, with a Parameter-Free Compression Baseline
item.description: Standardized re-evaluation of causal direction detection methods reveals zero-parameter compression baseline rivals state-of-the-art when eliminating tuning bias.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Deciphering Fingerprints of 3D Molecular Surfaces for Accurate Epitope Prediction
item.description: SurfBind improves epitope prediction by operating directly on 3D molecular surface representations via Transformers.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Reconstructing GRACE Terrestrial Water Storage with Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Networks: An Application to South America
item.description: Graph neural networks reconstruct terrestrial water storage back to 1940 using satellite observations and meteorological data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] The Degeneracy Distillery
item.description: Method detects and resolves degenerate parameters via Fisher information matrix analysis, yielding symbolic transformations and reduced simulation budgets.
